items.title: Undergraduate Admissions Deadlines
items.content: Fall Term: Early Action due Nov 1; Regular Decision due Jan 15. Spring Term: Rolling admissions closes Oct 15. Applications require transcripts, test-optional ACT/SAT, personal statement, and one recommendation.

items.title: Scholarships & Financial Aid Overview
items.content: Merit scholarships are automatically considered with application; need-based aid requires FAFSA and institutional profile by Feb 1. Renewal requires maintaining a 3.0 GPA and full-time enrollment.

items.title: Academic Calendar Highlights
items.content: Fall semester runs late Aug–Dec; Spring runs Jan–May. Midterms in Oct and Mar. Final exam weeks are Dec and May. Official holidays: Labor Day, Thanksgiving, Winter Break, Spring Break.

items.title: Faculty Research Support
items.content: Seed grants up to $25,000 available each Fall; IRB required for human-subjects research. Grant development workshops held monthly; internal deadlines are two weeks before sponsor due dates.

items.title: Staff HR Policies (Leave & Benefits)
items.content: Full-time staff accrue 1.25 days PTO per month. Health, dental, and vision become active after 30 days. Remote work up to 2 days/week with supervisor approval.

items.title: Campus Tours & Visitor Info
items.content: Campus tours run Mon–Fri at 10am and 2pm; Saturday at 11am. Register online at /visit. Visitor parking available in Garage A; validation at Welcome Center.

items.title: AI Policy Introduction
items.content: Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies are rapidly evolving and present both significant opportunities and challenges within the academic environment. K J Somaiya Institute of Management recognizes the potential of AI tools to enhance learning, research, and administrative processes when used ethically and responsibly. This policy provides guidelines for students regarding the acceptable use of AI tools in their academic pursuits, aligning with the Institute's commitment to academic integrity, intellectual growth, and the development of responsible future leaders in business and technology. These guidelines are subject to review and adaptation as AI technology and regulatory landscapes evolve. This policy reflects global best practices and Indian regulatory standards, including full compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, to ensure that AI use aligns with our core values of academic integrity, ethical practice, inclusivity, and responsible innovation.

items.title: General Guidelines for AI Tool Use
items.content: The use of AI tools in academic work at K J Somaiya Institute of Management is governed by the following principles: AI as a Co-Thinker/Enhancer, Not a Substitute: AI tools may support brainstorming, clarifying concepts, improving writing, or suggesting structures. However, students must remain the primary authors of their work. Ethical AI use means engaging critically with the output, revising it meaningfully, and applying personal judgment and creativity. All AI-assisted work must reflect the user's original thinking, with appropriate acknowledgment of AI contributions. Academic Integrity and Accountability: Work submitted must reflect the student's original understanding. Submitting AI-generated content without substantial independent contribution or proper acknowledgment constitutes academic misconduct. Students are solely responsible for verifying and explaining all AI-assisted content, including factual accuracy, logic, and data interpretation. Mandatory Disclosure: Students must transparently disclose any AI tool used in their academic work, regardless of how minor the assistance. This includes specifying: a. The AI tool(s) used (e.g., ChatGPT, Grammarly, GitHub Copilot). b. The purpose (e.g., outlining, summarizing, grammar correction). c. Any significant edits, revisions, or validations made to AI-generated content. d. Optionally, relevant prompts for complex interactions. Example Disclosure: 'AI Assistance: ChatGPT 4 was used to brainstorm section headings ('Suggest themes for a report on X') and Grammarly AI was used for grammar suggestions. All AI-generated content was critically reviewed and revised by the author.' Citation of AI-Generated Content: When AI is used to generate specific quoted content (e.g., images, text), it must be cited following a style guide (APA, MLA, etc.), adapted for AI. Example: (OpenAI's DALL-E, 2023) or 'Conclusion informed by Google Bard (October 2023 version).' Privacy, Security & Legal Compliance: In accordance with India's DPDP Act, 2023, no confidential, sensitive, or personally identifiable information (PII) shall be entered into public-facing AI tools without explicit consent and Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA), where applicable. Instructor and Team Considerations: Individual faculty may impose stricter rules. Students must comply with course-specific guidelines. In group projects, all members must consent to AI use and be informed of how AI tools were applied. Cautious Use of AI Agents: Be mindful when interacting with autonomous AI agents capable of performing multi step tasks or executing actions independently (e.g., Auto-GPT, AgentGPT). Use such tools only when explicitly permitted and with full understanding of their limitations, potential biases, and institutional policies. Misuse Prevention: AI must not be used to fabricate content, mislead, or harm. Students must not rely on AI to circumvent learning or gain unfair academic advantage. Responsible AI use upholds fairness, integrity, and the Institute's values.

items.title: Do's and Don'ts of AI Tool Usage
items.content: The following table provides examples of generally acceptable and unacceptable uses of AI tools. These may be further refined by specific course instructors. Do's: Using AI for brainstorming initial ideas, topics, or perspectives. Using AI for outlining or structuring assignments, papers, or presentations. Using AI to clarify complex concepts or summarize material you have already read and understood. Using AI to generate code snippets, formulas, or initial data analysis steps, provided you fully understand the output, verify its correctness, and can explain it. Using AI for practice, such as generating practice questions or scenarios for presentations or interviews or receiving feedback on drafts. Using AI to identify relevant academic papers, research methodologies, or datasets (always access and critically evaluate the original sources). Using AI tools in ways explicitly permitted or required by your instructor for specific assignments or learning activities. Using AI for basic data annotation, cleaning, or initial transformation steps, provided you understand the process, verify the results, and adhere to data privacy guidelines. Using AI to explore different analytical techniques or interpretations for data you have analyzed yourself. Don'ts: Submitting content (e.g., essays, reports, code, presentations) that was predominantly generated by an AI tool and presenting it as your own original work without proper disclosure or substantial independent contribution. Relying solely on AI to complete assignments without engaging with the course material, conducting your own research, or developing your own understanding. Presenting AI-generated information, data, or facts as accurate without verifying their accuracy from reliable, independent, and credible sources. Using AI to generate references, citations, or source lists without confirming their existence and accuracy. Failing to clearly and transparently disclose the use of AI tools as required by this policy or your instructor. Using AI in a manner that violates the Institute's Academic Integrity regulations, Honor Code, this policy, or any course-specific rules set by your instructor. Using AI to gain an unfair academic advantage over other students. Inputting sensitive, confidential, proprietary, or personal data into public-facing AI tools or platforms with unclear or inadequate privacy policies. Using AI to generate entirely fake data or manipulate real data without disclosing the manipulation.

items.title: Enforcement and Accountability Measures
items.content: To strengthen the academic integrity framework around AI use, the Institute will implement a tiered response system for AI-related violations, calibrated based on the severity and intent of misuse. Minor violations, such as failing to disclose the use of AI tools in permitted contexts, may result in a warning and mandatory resubmission with proper attribution. Moderate violations, including submitting AI-generated content without meaningful human engagement or critical analysis, may attract penalties such as grade reduction, academic probation, or requirement of remedial ethics training. Severe violations, such as using AI tools to cheat in assessments, fabricate research findings, or generate entire academic submissions dishonestly, will result in serious disciplinary action, including course failure, suspension, or expulsion. All reported cases will follow a fair appeals and grievance process, with the opportunity for the student or researcher to present context and evidence. To guide community understanding, the policy will include illustrative examples of unacceptable AI use: e.g., inserting fake citations generated by AI, passing off entire AI-generated research papers as original work, or employing AI to solve assignments in violation of examination rules. This structured approach aims to balance education, deterrence, and fairness, reinforcing the Institute's commitment to responsible and ethical AI integration.

items.title: Legal and Ethical Risks of AI
items.content: To proactively manage the legal and ethical risks associated with the use of Generative AI tools in academic and research settings, the Institute will adopt a comprehensive risk-awareness checklist for all users. This checklist will require users to evaluate: The risk of plagiarism when using AI-generated text or code, especially when content is reused without proper citation. The fair use of third-party intellectual property (IP), ensuring that AI tools are not used to replicate or manipulate copyrighted content without authorization. The potential for bias, stereotyping, or discriminatory outcomes, especially in AI-generated outputs related to datasets, language models, or decision-support systems. Users must demonstrate awareness of and compliance with India's Information Technology Act, Copyright Act, and the principles outlined in national guidelines such as India's Ethical AI Framework and DPDP Act, 2023. Special care should be taken when handling datasets involving personal data, protected identities, or culturally sensitive material. Training modules will include real-world case studies highlighting ethical lapses, copyright violations, and legal consequences resulting from the careless use of AI. By integrating this checklist and awareness framework into academic workflows, the Institute aims to promote responsible innovation grounded in legality, fairness, and ethical foresight.

items.title: AI in Research and Innovation
items.content: To support cutting-edge academic work while preserving ethical and scholarly standards, the Institute introduces a dedicated framework for the use of AI in research and innovation. Researchers are encouraged to responsibly integrate AI tools for data analysis, visualization, simulation, and exploratory modeling, particularly in theses, dissertations, and funded projects. However, all use must adhere to the principles of critical interpretation, transparency, and reproducibility. Students and faculty must explicitly declare how AI tools contributed to their research, including any generated code, summaries, models, or visual outputs. Additionally, all datasets used—especially those containing personal or sensitive data—must be ethically sourced, obtained with appropriate permissions or licenses, and aligned with data protection standards under the DPDP Act, 2023. Any code or AI models developed with external assistance must be cited properly, and reproducibility protocols (e.g., versioned code, model documentation) must be maintained. The Institute will also provide ethical review and Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) templates for AI-integrated research projects. This structured approach ensures that AI serves as a powerful enabler of innovation while upholding academic rigor and ethical responsibility.

items.title: AI Literacy and Capacity Building
items.content: To ensure meaningful, informed, and responsible engagement with artificial intelligence, the Institute will mandate AI Literacy Programs for all students, faculty, and research staff. These programs will be embedded into orientation sessions, teaching workshops, and ongoing faculty development initiatives. The curriculum will be designed to demystify how Generative AI works, covering the architecture and behavior of large language models (LLMs), and explaining their limitations. Modules will address key issues such as algorithmic bias, fairness in AI systems, and the phenomenon of AI hallucinations, with emphasis on the need for rigorous fact-checking and critical evaluation of AI-generated content. Additionally, participants will explore the environmental impact of AI, fostering awareness around AI and sustainability, including energy consumption, ethical sourcing of computational resources, and carbon footprint considerations. The Institute will also offer micro-courses and certification programs in ethical AI use, practical applications, and regulatory compliance, empowering stakeholders to responsibly integrate AI into teaching, research, and institutional functions.

items.title: AI Policy Review and Updates
items.content: The landscape of AI technology and its integration into education is rapidly evolving. This policy will be regularly reviewed and updated by the Institute, in consultation with faculty, students, and administration, to ensure it remains relevant, effective, and supportive of ethical and responsible AI use in the academic environment. Updates and revisions will be communicated to all stakeholders in a timely manner. The goal is to ensure that the indications in this policy effectively respond to changes, promoting a use of AI that is safe, inclusive, and in line with academic standards.

items.title: Team Selection and Representation Overview
items.content: The KJSIM Sports Committee, in coordination with the Somaiya Sports Academy (SSA), is solely responsible for the selection of KJSIM teams for inter-college sports tournaments. Official announcements regarding trials and selections will be communicated to the entire student body exclusively through the official email channel by the Sports Committee.

items.title: Sports Selection Process Requirements
items.content: Attendance at officially scheduled trials is mandatory for consideration. Students who fail to attend trials, regardless of prior experience or past representation, will not be eligible for selection.

items.title: Practice and Training Requirements for Athletes
items.content: Once selected, student-athletes are required to participate in mandatory training and practice sessions scheduled by the Sports Committee and respective teams. A minimum attendance of 75% at these sessions is required to maintain eligibility for the final playing squad. Consistent absences without prior intimation or valid reason will lead to removal from the contingent.

items.title: Sports Representation Protocol
items.content: For inter-college tournaments, the Sports Committee will receive official invitations from the host institute and submit the final contingent list. Team allocation for specific tournaments will follow this structure: Somaiya Games – Selection in collaboration with SSA. Yudh & Anti-Ragging Sports Fest – Selection exclusively by the KJSIM Sports Committee. Any student representing KJSIM or Somaiya Vidyavihar University (SVU) in any sporting event (internal or external) must: Inform the KJSIM Sports Committee prior to participation. Obtain formal approval before confirming attendance (In case of representing KJSIM).

items.title: Eligibility for Attendance Benefits
items.content: Attendance benefits will be granted only to students whose names appear on the official contingent list maintained by the Sports Committee. The Sports Committee will be the sole authority for verifying and authenticating participation records.

items.title: Sports Leave Application Procedure
items.content: Students seeking attendance for sports representation must: Apply for leave via the Somaiya App under the 'Apply Leave' section. Select either 'Apply by Date' or 'Apply by Lecture' as appropriate. In the reason field, select 'Participation in Sports'. In the explanation, specify: Name of the tournament. Name of the organizing institute. Attach valid proof of participation, which may include: Official contingent list issued by the Sports Committee. Official selection email for the respective tournament. Submit the application for review. Important: Leave applications without proper proof will not be approved. Blanket approvals for attendance will not be issued under any circumstances. The Chairperson of the Sports Committee will approve leave only after verifying the authenticity of the provided details.

items.title: Expected Standards of Conduct for Athletes
items.content: All students representing KJSIM are ambassadors of the institute and must uphold the highest standards of integrity, discipline, and sportsmanship. These standards apply: During matches and competitions. Throughout training sessions. During travel, stay, and interactions with other teams, officials, and spectators.

items.title: Prohibited Behaviors in Sports
items.content: Use of offensive or abusive language. Unsporting conduct, including dissent against referee decisions. Physical or verbal aggression towards players, officials, or spectators. Any action that tarnishes the reputation of KJSIM.

items.title: Disciplinary Consequences for Sports Violations
items.content: Violations of the Code of Conduct will attract strict action, including but not limited to: First Offense: Formal warning and possible match suspension. Repeated or Severe Offense: Temporary or permanent suspension from the team. Disqualification from future tournaments. Referral to the Institute Disciplinary Committee for further action. Final Authority: All disciplinary decisions will be made by the KJSIM Sports Committee in consultation with the designated faculty coordinator.

items.title: Intra-College Sports Participation Requirements
items.content: All students must register through the official registration process announced by the Sports Committee. Impersonation, misrepresentation, or participation under another student's name will result in immediate disqualification and possible disciplinary action.

items.title: Conduct and Fair Play in Tournaments
items.content: Participants must maintain respect towards teammates, opponents, referees, and event organizers at all times. Verbal abuse, violence, or disruptive conduct will not be tolerated. Referee and official decisions are final and binding. Protests during play are not permitted.

items.title: Disciplinary Measures for Sports Tournaments
items.content: Possible penalties include: Official warning. Match forfeiture. Suspension from the ongoing tournament. Ban from future internal and external tournaments.

items.title: General Provisions of Sports Policy
items.content: This policy applies to all students of KJSIM, regardless of program or year of study. The Sports Committee reserves the right to interpret, amend, or update this policy as required. Students are expected to familiarize themselves with these guidelines and comply fully. Ignorance of the policy will not be accepted as a defense in disciplinary matters.
